notes: Fixed-effect log-link mu; single-seed n=1800 fit recovers beta_mu within tolerance 0.12 and confint() returns Wald CIs with correct fixef:mu labels (conf.status=="wald"). Not a multi-seed coverage study, so this stops short of inference_ready_with_caveats.
drm_validate_reml_spec() aborts for any model_type other than gaussian/biv_gaussian; applies uniformly to every dpar/effect_type/provider/q_gate for zi_nbinom2.
Fixed-effect log-link overdispersion sigma; same single-seed fit recovers beta_sigma within tolerance 0.18 and confint() returns Wald fixef:sigma rows.
Same model-wide REML gate as mu.

Fixed-effect logit-link zero-inflation probability; same single-seed fit recovers beta_zi within tolerance 0.45 and confint() returns Wald fixef:zi rows. Note: emmeans() explicitly rejects zi_nbinom2 fits (tests/testthat/test-emmeans-methods.R:790-799), a separate methods-coverage gap not captured by these axes.
Same model-wide REML gate.

count ~ x + (1|id) with any zi formula aborts: "mu random intercepts and slopes are implemented only for ordinary models". src/drmTMB.cpp model_type==9 (3419-3477) has no u_mu/mu_re_term application either, confirming no engine-level path.
Rejected independently by the mu-RE gate (fires first, during spec building) and by the model-wide REML gate.
validate_poisson_mu_random_terms() does not distinguish intercept vs slope when has_zi=TRUE; "count ~ x + (0+x|id)" aborts identically.
Same double rejection as the intercept row.
sigma ~ z + (1|id) with any zi formula aborts: "NB2 sigma random intercepts are implemented only for ordinary NB2 models".
Double rejection.
The has_zi check fires before the intercept-only type check, so a slope term is caught by the same message; ordinary (non-zi) NB2 sigma RE is intercept-only anyway.
Double rejection.
zi ~ x + (1|id) aborts: "[zero-inflation] random effects are not implemented." No zi random-effect design (Z/u) exists in src/drmTMB.cpp for model_type==9.
Double rejection.
The "|" bar-term check does not distinguish intercept vs slope.
Double rejection.

Only unlabelled intercept-only spatial(1|site,coords=coords) mu field is admitted when a zi formula is present; sigma must stay fixed-effect-only and mu must carry no ordinary RE terms. Board row: fit_status=point_fit, interval_status=unsupported, coverage_status=unsupported, denominator_policy=no_denominator_local_debug_only -- a single local deterministic smoke fit, not a retained-denominator recovery study. Tests confirm convergence, pdHess, sdpars$mu, ranef("spatial_mu"), and a direct profile_targets() row, but this is not interval, coverage, inference_ready, or supported evidence.

evidence_source: R/drmTMB.R:7314,7358-7365 (intercept-only check on the zi-admitted marker); tests/testthat/test-count-structured-mu.R:723-733 (expect_error "intercept gate")
notes: One-slope variant "spatial(1+x|site,...)" mu with any zi formula is rejected: "structured mu effects currently support only the first spatial intercept gate." This is the slope_class=independent_one_slope sibling of the implemented row above; only intercept_only (q1) is admitted for zi_nbinom2.
Model-wide REML gate blocks every structured-mu combination (any provider, slope class, or q_gate) for zi_nbinom2 before the structured-mu validator is ever reached under REML.

Representative row, same gate as phylo. Ordinary (non-zi) NB2 sibling is implemented (board cell_id qseries_phylo_interaction_nbinom2_q1_mu).
Representative row, same gate. Ordinary (non-zi) sibling implemented (board cell_id qseries_animal_nbinom2_q1_mu_intercept).
Representative row, same gate. Ordinary (non-zi) sibling implemented (board cell_id qseries_relmat_nbinom2_q1_mu_intercept).

notes: Representative row covering phylo, phylo_interaction, spatial, animal, and relmat in the zi formula: drm_build_nbinom2_spec() never extracts a structured zi term (contrast drm_build_poisson_spec()'s zi_spatial extraction at R/drmTMB.R:5270-5275, which lets zi_poisson admit zi ~ spatial(...); see tests/testthat/test-nongaussian-structured-boundary.R:373-381), so any structured marker in zi ~ ... hits the generic "Structured-effect syntax is planned, not implemented" abort before any zi-specific logic runs.
